theater_description: Campaign-level NATO air war against targets across the Federal Republic of Yugoslavia, including Serbia, Montenegro, and Kosovo. Civilian exposure is modeled at the campaign-wide FRY level because the civilian fatality source and the target geography are both campaign-wide rather than local to one city.
geometry_method_note: FRY-wide civilian denominator is explicitly justified by campaign-wide target geography and by the HRW civilian casualty dataset covering incidents across Serbia, Montenegro, and Kosovo.
military_area_note: Military denominator is a bounded official-force scenario using active Yugoslav Army strength plus a narrowly grounded special-police component. It is not a complete census of every police, reserve, or irregular formation exposed during the campaign.
civilian_area_note: Civilian denominator starts from the July 1999 FRY population estimate and applies scenario-bounded displacement adjustments tied to the documented wartime expulsion of Kosovo Albanians.
split_reason: No city-level split was retained because the strongest civilian numerator and the recoverable force-exposure logic are both campaign-wide.
quality_note: Civilian deaths are well researched, but the military denominator is a conservative scenario floor and the military deaths series is retrospective and partly dependent on official commemorative reporting.
